page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 32 shared x86_64 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. The underlying hardware features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.0 GB per core. The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core and memory performance alongside weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10% with a score of 748.19, and single-threaded gzip compression is weak at 23.67 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded operations scale effectively, as demonstrated by a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 228.74 GB/sec and an average Geekbench multi-core score of 10107.0. Memory bandwidth is consistent, reaching 131.44 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 82.64 GB/sec for large block write operations.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a high volume of RAM relative to its shared CPU allocation, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated single-core CPU performance. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the relatively low 2.0 GHz clock speed, which limits its suitability for compute-bound or single-threaded tasks.

This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale caching, in-memory database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate web serving. It is also capable of handling basic LLM inference tasks, achieving 92.06 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 10107.0 and a strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 228.74 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance is limited, as reflected in a poor stress-ng single-core score of 748.19. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that can tolerate shared CPU allocation. It is suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate web serving.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is a KVM virtual server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, offering high memory density for multi-threaded workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 KVM server with 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring thirty-two shared AMD EPYC cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory (12.0 GB per core)
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance of 228.74 GB/sec
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and multi-threaded processing
